suppressions.reason: Architecture plugin is intentionally centralized so all custom oxlint rules are loaded as one JS plugin.
Current pnpm audit reports transitive ws moderate advisory; note and avoid dependency upgrades until compatibility is reviewed.
getAuthOptions is pure Better Auth config construction; structurally required to assemble all plugin options in one call.
Well-known alias handlers follow identical audience-load plus auth-handler pattern by design; each alias resolves a different pathname.
Route map entries are structurally identical contract records listing name/method/path/source; duplication is intentional documentation.
Plugin endpoint handlers follow identical pattern: findOne, check NOT_FOUND, assert access, update/delete, invalidate cache. Structural similarity is intentional in BA plugin endpoints.
Auth schema is generated/maintained as the single Better Auth and plugin table contract; splitting it would create schema drift.
OAuth scope catalog Better Auth endpoints are intentionally explicit and mirror resource-server plugin endpoint shape: validate, authorize, mutate through BA adapter, invalidate cache.
Create/update payload builders for scope and grant rows intentionally share timestamp and actor-stamping structure while preserving explicit row types.
vite is imported in configuration and resolved through transitive monorepo dependencies.
_styles import is required to load global css while complying with oxlint unassigned-import rules.
Comment explains the cross-reference design contract of OAuth query parameters.
False positive on getpass prompt text; no password or secret value is hardcoded.
Better Auth plugin endpoint handlers intentionally share explicit validate-authorize-mutate-invalidate structure across plugin-owned CRUD surfaces.
SCIM directory plugin centralizes all endpoint registrations for the SCIM v2 read-only surface; splitting routes would fragment the plugin contract.
Comment links userName eq filter handler to findUser for future coupling awareness when userName is upgraded to email.
Comment documents KV getAndDelete non-atomic limitation per SEC-008 audit finding.
Comment documents rate-limit strategy per SEC-003: workers_dev: false is the primary defense; WAF rules handle route throttling.
JSDoc at architectural boundary documenting the privacy rule for userName mapping.
JSDoc at architectural boundary documenting OpenAPI content-type limitation for SCIM+JSON.
TODO comment at architectural boundary tracking Better Auth content-type support for SCIM OpenAPI docs.
Import block and icon map literal both enumerate the same icon names; explicit one-to-one name-to-component registry mapping is structurally required like mapper files.
Two NavLink render blocks (top-level items and group children) follow identical active-icon-link pattern across different data sources; structurally analogous to route-file duplication.
Intentionally omits props.page from debounce effect deps — only search value drives the debounce reset; adding props.page would cause the offset to reset on page navigation.
Mock user fixture objects must explicitly list all User fields per type; structural repetition is required to make each fixture independently readable in stories.
Mock invitation fixture objects intentionally list all Invitation fields for each status so story data remains independently readable.
Shared UI Avatar accepts arbitrary user-supplied image URLs across Vinext/Ladle contexts; Next Image optimization and remote allowlists are not appropriate at this primitive boundary.
Comment points to docs/024 explaining that admin-login MFA is enforced server-side by id-admin-sign-in-guard; account-level TOTP and session-freshness remain documented future work.

assertOtpGenerateLimit/assertOtpVerifyLimit are intentional named bindings that keep KV key prefixes, attempt limits, and TTLs co-located in operations.ts rather than leaking those constants into the index.ts guard handler.

Better Auth does not export a precise type for ctx.context inside a hooks.before matcher; the narrowed AdminSignInGuardContext assertion mirrors the established pattern in oauth-m2m-bridge (ctx.context.adapter as unknown as ...).
DaisyUI and React Aria link comments are required on every packages/ui component file — all existing components use the same pattern.
Story files have structurally similar filter group configurations by design — each story sets up the same filter groups with different initial values.
ResizeObserver overflow measurement logic is inherently verbose; splitting would scatter sibling DOM reads/writes across helpers and obscure the collapse/expand algorithm.
Empty catch block is standard no-flash theme initialization: localStorage might be unavailable in opaque origins; fallback to default theme is intentional and safe.
Inline script content is a static theme-initialization string with no user-controlled input; same pattern as Next.js theme-flash-prevention examples.
